domain: Mau Piailug Polynesian wayfinding (star compass 32-house fixed celestial reference + persistent ocean swell pattern + cross-canoe visibility correction + single trained navigator per canoe + cross-fleet knowledge relay)
specific_mechanism: Fleet shares a fixed celestial reference (star compass houses); each canoe has its own persistent swell-pattern signal decoupled from immediate visibility; cross-canoe visibility yields drift-correction; one trained navigator per canoe owns the consensus.
llm_application: PIAILUG-FLEET: a multi-agent LLM system in which all K agents share a FIXED embedded reference axis (e.g., a frozen direction-vector in latent space serving as 'celestial north') that is NEVER updated during a session. Each agent maintains its own persistent low-frequency context-summary (swell-pattern analog) decoupled from immediate input. Agents periodically run a cross-agent visibility check: project each agent's hidden state onto the fixed reference axis, compute pairwise drift, apply consensus correction. A designated 'navigator' agent per cluster owns the decision and relays to peer clusters.
novelty_claim: Existing LLM multi-agent systems (CAMON, CONSENSAGENT, Multi-Agent Consensus Seeking, debate / round-table protocols) use peer-to-peer messaging or majority-vote; none use a FIXED FROZEN REFERENCE AXIS as the anchor for drift correction. PIAILUG-FLEET differs by frozen-axis projection + persistent low-frequency context + cluster-navigator relay structure. The frozen reference axis is the key novelty (vs adaptive consensus).
